doc.title: Model comparison & pipeline
doc.description: Side-by-side params/BLEU table plus the encoder–decoder data flow.

blocks.text: A compact comparison of the **Transformer** against the recurrent and convolutional baselines it replaced, followed by the high-level data flow through the architecture.

blocks.caption: WMT 2014 English-to-German: quality vs. training cost.

blocks.text: The encoder maps the input to continuous representations; the decoder attends over them while auto-regressively emitting tokens:

blocks.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  I[Input tokens] --> E[Encoder stack x6]
  E --> A[Cross-attention]
  A --> D[Decoder stack x6]
  D --> O[Output probabilities]
blocks.caption: End-to-end data flow through the Transformer.

blocks.mermaid:
xychart-beta
  title "BLEU by model"
  x-axis [GNMT, ConvS2S, "T-base", "T-big"]
  y-axis "BLEU" 22 --> 29
  bar [24.6, 25.16, 27.3, 28.4]

blocks.title: Quality vs. training cost
blocks.caption: BLEU against training FLOPs (log x) — the Transformer is both better and cheaper.
blocks.alt: Scatter plot of BLEU versus training FLOPs on a log x-axis; Transformer points sit upper-left of the recurrent/convolutional baselines.
